# Agent0-VL: Exploring Self-Evolving Agent for Tool-Integrated Vision-Language Reasoning

## TL;DR
Agent0-VL is a single large vision-language model (LVLM) that improves itself with zero external reward by alternating between a Solver role (multi-turn tool-integrated reasoning) and a Verifier role (tool-grounded critique that emits step-level rewards), yielding a 12.5% average gain over the Qwen2.5-VL-7B base across seven visual reasoning benchmarks.

## Key findings
- **Core idea**: extend tool-integrated reasoning (TIR) from the *solving* step into *self-evaluation* and *self-repair*. The model can re-invoke tools (e.g. Python sandbox) while critiquing its own reasoning, replacing purely text-based self-reward — which suffers "evaluation hallucination" (rewarding linguistically plausible but visually wrong answers).
- **Unified dual-role policy** πθ with a role indicator m ∈ {Solver, Verifier}, sharing parameters. The Verifier emits a tuple Vₜ = (scoreₜ ∈ [−1,1], confₜ ∈ [0,1], critiqueₜ) per reasoning step.
- **Self-Evolving Reasoning Cycle (SERC)**: an inner loop generates trajectories + step-level verification + confidence-gated repair; an outer loop updates the shared policy via **GRPO** (Group Relative Policy Optimization). Confidence-gated repair fires when confₜ < τc (set to 0.7), using a sigmoid gate gₜ = σ(κ(τc − confₜ)); unnecessary repairs are penalized.
- **Performance (7B)**: average 65.6 vs base 58.3 (+12.5%). MathVista 67.8→75.6, MathVision 25.1→37.3, HallusionBench 65.0→72.9, ChartQA 83.5→87.3. Math-related benchmarks improved +18.1%; HallusionBench +12.2% (visual hallucination reduction). Beats ThinkLite-VL-7B by 4.29% avg.
- **Stronger base (Qwen3-VL-8B)**: Agent0-VL-8B reaches 74.6 avg, +6.1% over base and surpassing GPT-4o on MathVista, HallBench, ChartQA.
- **Monotonic iterative gains**: Iter 1/2/3 = +5.2%, +4.0%, +2.8% cumulative over base (Table 2).
- **Verifier as standalone Process Reward Model (PRM)**: scoring Best-of-8 trajectories from *other* LVLMs (Qwen2.5-VL-3B → 32B, InternVL) gives an average +7.3% (Table 4, Fig 3) — generalizes beyond its own policy.
- **Ablations (Table 3)**: removing SERC (SFT-only) is the largest drop (−8.7% avg); removing tool use −6.5%; removing self-repair −2.5%.

## Methods (brief)
Two-stage training on Qwen2.5-VL-7B and Qwen3-VL-8B: (1) SFT cold-start on ~200k tool-augmented reasoning/verification/repair trajectories (auto-generated by GPT-5 + Qwen2.5-VL-72B teachers, sandbox-validated and filtered); (2) a short external-reward RL warm-up (2 epochs) to stabilize tool use, then self-evolving RL on ~40k tasks driven purely by internal process rewards. GRPO with group size N=8, βKL=0.001, on 8×H200 GPUs. Benchmarks: MathVerse, MathVision, MathVista, WeMath, MMMU, HallusionBench, ChartQA.

## Limitations
Evaluated only on visual math / chart / science benchmarks (7 datasets); gains shrink across iterations (+5.2→+2.8%), suggesting saturation. The "zero external reward" claim is qualified — a 2-epoch external-reward RL warm-up precedes self-evolution. No biological or chemistry domain evaluation; generalization to non-math visual reasoning untested.

## Citations of interest
- Shao et al. 2024 (DeepSeekMath / GRPO, [44]) — the relative-advantage RL optimizer used in the outer loop.
- Xia et al. 2025, "Agent0" ([62]) — the text-only predecessor this work extends to vision + tool-grounded verification.
- Wang et al. 2509.25541, "Vision-Zero" ([55]) — game-based VLM self-play baseline it compares against.
- Zhou et al. NeurIPS 2024, "Calibrated Self-Rewarding VLMs" ([81]) — motivates the evaluation-hallucination problem in text-only self-reward.
- Yao et al. ICLR 2023, "ReAct" ([69]) — foundational reasoning-and-acting tool-use paradigm.
- Zuo et al. 2025, "TTRL: Test-Time RL" ([85]) — self-consistency-as-reward precedent for label-free training.
